test_cases.name: happy_path_standard_product_launch
test_cases.scenario: User provides a clear product launch goal with context, resources, and risks; skill produces complete 90-day plan with all phases executed.

input.goal_statement: Launch a new SaaS product line for enterprise customers
input.goal_context: Current state: prototype exists, no customers. Desired state: 10 paying customers, product in production. Stakeholders: CEO, VP Product, VP Engineering.

test_cases.expected_behavior: Skill executes all 8 phases, producing clarified goal with measurable success criteria, 3-5 KRAs with owners and readiness assessments, three dated milestones with deliverables and acceptance criteria, 13 weeks of tasks with effort estimates and dependencies, dependency graph with critical path <= 90 days, resource allocation showing utilization per week, checkpoint structure with metrics and escalation criteria, risk register with mitigation strategies, and complete markdown action plan document.
phase_coverage: Phase 1: Clarify and Validate the Goal
Phase 2: Identify Key Result Areas and Outcomes
Phase 3: Define Milestones at 30, 60, and 90 Days
Phase 4: Break Milestones into Weekly Tasks
Phase 5: Map Dependencies and Identify Critical Path
Phase 6: Assign Resources and Build Contingency
Phase 7: Define Weekly Checkpoints and Escalation Criteria
Phase 8: Assemble and Validate the 90-Day Plan

test_cases.name: happy_path_minimal_resources
test_cases.scenario: User provides goal with minimal resources (single person, 40 hours/week); skill produces realistic plan with appropriate scope and contingency.

input.goal_context: Current state: team reports low morale and unclear priorities. Desired state: measurable 30% productivity increase, team satisfaction score >4/5.

test_cases.expected_behavior: Skill assumes single-person execution with 40 hours/week and standard tools. Produces plan with realistic scope for one person, identifies KRAs (e.g., Process Improvement, Team Engagement, Tools/Systems), allocates 10-15% contingency, and flags team resistance as a key risk with mitigation strategy.
phase_coverage: Phase 1: Clarify and Validate the Goal
Phase 2: Identify Key Result Areas and Outcomes
Phase 3: Define Milestones at 30, 60, and 90 Days
Phase 4: Break Milestones into Weekly Tasks
Phase 6: Assign Resources and Build Contingency
Phase 7: Define Weekly Checkpoints and Escalation Criteria

test_cases.name: edge_case_ambiguous_goal
test_cases.scenario: User provides vague goal statement ('be better', 'improve things'); skill clarifies by asking for specific current state, desired state, and measurable gap.
test_cases.expected_behavior: Phase 1 detects ambiguity and produces clarified_goal with rewritten SMART statement (e.g., 'Reduce average response time from 24 hours to 4 hours and achieve 95% first-contact resolution rate'). Includes explicit assumptions about current metrics and validation methods.
phase_coverage: Phase 1: Clarify and Validate the Goal

test_cases.name: edge_case_oversized_goal
test_cases.scenario: User provides goal too large for 90 days ('build a company', 'transform entire organization'); skill scopes down to a 90-day milestone and defers rest to future phases.

input.goal_statement: Build a fully functional SaaS company from scratch
input.goal_context: Starting with idea only, no team, no funding yet.

test_cases.expected_behavior: Phase 1 or Phase 2 detects oversized goal and adjusts scope. Clarified goal becomes '90-day milestone: complete MVP with 3 founding team members and secure seed funding conversations'. Deferred items documented (full product, customer acquisition at scale, profitability). Plan focuses on achievable 90-day outcomes.
phase_coverage: Phase 1: Clarify and Validate the Goal
Phase 2: Identify Key Result Areas and Outcomes

test_cases.name: edge_case_single_kra
test_cases.scenario: Goal is simple enough to have only 1-2 KRAs; skill either consolidates into a checklist or identifies why 3-5 KRAs are not feasible.

input.goal_context: External auditor scheduled for day 85. Must prepare all documentation and remediate findings.

test_cases.expected_behavior: Phase 2 identifies 2-3 KRAs (Documentation Preparation, Finding Remediation, Audit Readiness). Skill notes that goal is simpler than typical 90-day plan but still produces full structure. Milestones align with audit date (day 85 = final readiness checkpoint).
phase_coverage: Phase 1: Clarify and Validate the Goal
Phase 2: Identify Key Result Areas and Outcomes
Phase 3: Define Milestones at 30, 60, and 90 Days
Phase 4: Break Milestones into Weekly Tasks

test_cases.name: error_handling_infeasible_timeline
test_cases.scenario: Skill calculates critical path > 90 days; returns error and suggests scope reduction, parallelization, or timeline extension.

input.goal_statement: Build and launch a mobile app with 50+ features
input.goal_context: Team of 2 engineers, 1 designer. No existing codebase.

test_cases.expected_behavior: Phase 5 detects critical path > 90 days (e.g., 120+ days for 50 features). Skill aborts plan generation and returns error: 'Plan is infeasible. Critical path duration exceeds 90 days. Recommend: (1) Reduce scope to 15-20 MVP features, (2) Add resources, or (3) Extend timeline to 120 days.' Suggests returning to Phase 3 or 4.
phase_coverage: Phase 5: Map Dependencies and Identify Critical Path

test_cases.name: error_handling_resource_bottleneck
test_cases.scenario: Skill detects weeks where utilization > 100% and cannot be resolved; returns error with mitigation options (defer, add resources, extend timeline).

input.goal_statement: Migrate legacy system to cloud and train team
input.goal_context: Current system running on-premises. Must migrate by day 90 with zero downtime.

test_cases.expected_behavior: Phase 6 detects weeks 4-8 with utilization > 100% (DevOps Lead over-allocated). Skill returns error: 'Resource bottleneck detected in weeks 4-8. DevOps Lead is over-allocated. Mitigation options: (1) Defer non-critical training to post-migration, (2) Add contract DevOps engineer, (3) Extend timeline to 120 days.' Requires stakeholder decision before proceeding.
phase_coverage: Phase 6: Assign Resources and Build Contingency

test_cases.name: quality_gate_boundary_effort_distribution
test_cases.scenario: Skill produces milestones with effort exactly at boundary (day 30 = 30%, day 60 = 70%, day 90 = 100%); verifies gate passes.

input.goal_statement: Launch marketing campaign with 3 channels (email, social, paid ads)
input.goal_context: Current state: no campaign infrastructure. Desired state: 10,000 leads generated, 5% conversion to trial.

test_cases.expected_behavior: Phase 3 produces milestones with effort distribution: day 30 = 30% (email infrastructure + initial content), day 60 = 70% (all channels live + first optimization), day 90 = 100% (full campaign + lead nurturing). Quality gate verifies: effort sums correctly, acceptance criteria are testable (e.g., 'Email list of 5,000 subscribers', 'First 100 leads captured'), critical path KRAs identified. Gate PASSES.
phase_coverage: Phase 3: Define Milestones at 30, 60, and 90 Days

test_cases.name: quality_gate_boundary_contingency_allocation
test_cases.scenario: Skill allocates contingency at exactly 10% and 15% boundaries; verifies gate passes and contingency is distributed across KRAs.

input.goal_statement: Implement new HR system (recruiting, onboarding, payroll)
input.goal_context: Current state: manual processes, spreadsheets. Desired state: automated workflows, 50% reduction in HR admin time.

test_cases.expected_behavior: Phase 3 and Phase 6 calculate total effort across KRAs (e.g., 1,200 hours). Allocate contingency: 10-15% = 120-180 hours. Distribute across KRAs: Recruiting (30 hours), Onboarding (40 hours), Payroll (50 hours), Training (20 hours). Quality gate verifies: contingency_hours_allocated = 140 (within 10-15% range), distributed to all KRAs, assigned to weeks before critical-path tasks. Gate PASSES.
phase_coverage: Phase 3: Define Milestones at 30, 60, and 90 Days
Phase 6: Assign Resources and Build Contingency

test_cases.name: quality_gate_boundary_critical_path_90_days
test_cases.scenario: Skill calculates critical path exactly at 90 days (or 88-92 days with acceptable variance); verifies gate passes.

input.goal_statement: Establish new regional office with team of 20
input.goal_context: Current state: headquarters only. Desired state: regional office operational with 20 employees, local partnerships established.

test_cases.expected_behavior: Phase 5 calculates critical path: identify longest chain (e.g., Real Estate Lease -> Office Setup -> Hiring -> Onboarding -> Go-Live). Sum effort on critical path: ~720 hours / 8 hours per day = 90 days. Quality gate verifies: critical_path_duration_days = 90 (acceptable), no circular dependencies, slack time calculated for non-critical tasks, parallelizable groups identified (e.g., hiring and office setup can run in parallel). Gate PASSES.
phase_coverage: Phase 5: Map Dependencies and Identify Critical Path

phases.phase: Phase 1: Clarify and Validate the Goal
Phase 2: Identify Key Result Areas and Outcomes
Phase 3: Define Milestones at 30, 60, and 90 Days
Phase 4: Break Milestones into Weekly Tasks
Phase 5: Map Dependencies and Identify Critical Path
Phase 6: Assign Resources and Build Contingency
Phase 7: Define Weekly Checkpoints and Escalation Criteria
Phase 8: Assemble and Validate the 90-Day Plan

test_rationale.name: happy_path_standard_product_launch
test_rationale.why_it_exists: Validates that the skill can execute all 8 phases end-to-end with realistic, multi-person team and clear goal. This is the primary success path.
test_rationale.what_it_tests: Complete workflow: goal clarification, KRA decomposition, milestone definition, task breakdown, dependency mapping, resource allocation, checkpoint design, risk management, and plan assembly.
test_rationale.failure_it_catches: Any phase that fails to produce required outputs, missing fields in outputs, inconsistent data across phases (e.g., task not traceable to milestone), critical path > 90 days, resource over-allocation, missing risk mitigation.

test_rationale.name: happy_path_minimal_resources
test_rationale.why_it_exists: Validates that the skill handles the default case (single person, 40 hours/week) gracefully and produces realistic scope.
test_rationale.what_it_tests: Default resource assumption, realistic scope for one person, appropriate contingency allocation, risk identification for team-based goals with single executor.
test_rationale.failure_it_catches: Skill assumes multi-person team when resources are null, over-allocates single person, fails to adjust scope for minimal resources, missing contingency.

test_rationale.name: edge_case_ambiguous_goal
test_rationale.why_it_exists: Validates Phase 1 error handling: skill must clarify vague goals by asking for current state, desired state, and measurable gap.
test_rationale.what_it_tests: Ambiguity detection, SMART rewriting, assumption extraction, validation method definition.
test_rationale.failure_it_catches: Skill accepts vague goal without clarification, produces unmeasurable success criteria, fails to identify assumptions, missing validation methods.

test_rationale.name: edge_case_oversized_goal
test_rationale.why_it_exists: Validates Phase 1-2 error handling: skill must scope down oversized goals to 90-day milestones and defer rest.
test_rationale.what_it_tests: Scope reduction, milestone extraction from larger goal, deferral documentation, realistic KRA count.
test_rationale.failure_it_catches: Skill attempts to plan full company build in 90 days, produces >5 KRAs, fails to identify deferred work, critical path > 90 days.

test_rationale.name: edge_case_single_kra
test_rationale.why_it_exists: Validates Phase 2 error handling: skill handles simple goals with 1-2 KRAs, either consolidating to checklist or explaining why full plan is still useful.
test_rationale.what_it_tests: KRA count flexibility, scope simplicity handling, plan structure for simple goals.
test_rationale.failure_it_catches: Skill forces 3-5 KRAs when goal is simple, produces artificial decomposition, fails to note that goal is simpler than typical.

test_rationale.name: error_handling_infeasible_timeline
test_rationale.why_it_exists: Validates Phase 5 error handling: skill must detect critical path > 90 days and abort with mitigation options.
test_rationale.what_it_tests: Critical path calculation, infeasibility detection, error messaging, mitigation suggestions (scope reduction, parallelization, timeline extension).
test_rationale.failure_it_catches: Skill produces plan with critical path > 90 days without error, fails to suggest mitigation, continues to Phase 6-8 with infeasible plan.

test_rationale.name: error_handling_resource_bottleneck
test_rationale.why_it_exists: Validates Phase 6 error handling: skill must detect over-allocation and return error with mitigation options.
test_rationale.what_it_tests: Utilization calculation, bottleneck detection (>100%), error messaging, mitigation options (defer, add resources, extend timeline).
test_rationale.failure_it_catches: Skill produces plan with weeks >100% utilization without flagging, fails to suggest mitigation, continues without stakeholder decision.

test_rationale.name: quality_gate_boundary_effort_distribution
test_rationale.why_it_exists: Validates Phase 3 quality gate: effort must be distributed 30-35% by day 30, 60-70% by day 60, 100% by day 90.
test_rationale.what_it_tests: Milestone effort allocation, acceptance criteria specificity, deliverable clarity, critical path KRA identification.
test_rationale.failure_it_catches: Effort distribution outside boundaries (e.g., 50% by day 30), vague acceptance criteria (e.g., 'looks good'), missing critical path KRAs, unmeasurable deliverables.

test_rationale.name: quality_gate_boundary_contingency_allocation
test_rationale.why_it_exists: Validates Phase 3 and Phase 6 quality gates: contingency must be 10-15% of total effort and distributed across all KRAs.
test_rationale.what_it_tests: Contingency calculation, distribution across KRAs, allocation to weeks before critical-path tasks.
test_rationale.failure_it_catches: Contingency outside 10-15% range, allocated to single KRA only, allocated to end of plan, missing from risk mitigation.

test_rationale.name: quality_gate_boundary_critical_path_90_days
test_rationale.why_it_exists: Validates Phase 5 quality gate: critical path must be <= 90 days (with acceptable variance 88-92 days).
test_rationale.what_it_tests: Critical path calculation, task dependency mapping, slack time calculation, parallelizable task identification.
test_rationale.failure_it_catches: Critical path > 92 days, circular dependencies, incorrect slack calculation, missed parallelization opportunities, infeasible plan.
